_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description:
You can:
- ensure technical architecture, including programme platforms and shared infrastructure, reflects the technical structures needed to achieve the organisation's objectives
- ensure the organisation uses and shares the appropriate technology architecture at the appropriate time
- make architectural decisions for the organisation taking into account costs, functionality, skills and other factors
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- working with different areas of the organisation to ensure the architecture works for everyone and any change is accepted
- convincing teams to exchange some autonomy for better functioning as an organisation
- overseeing decisions on technical architecture to ensure the best use of technology for the organisation
reference_document_element_description: Ability to select appropriate technical tools, languages, and platforms for data and AI requirements, evaluating trade-offs between performance, maintainability, team capability, ecosystem maturity, and cost.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-A-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-004 (TF, cosine: 0.475), DA-T-003 (DA, cosine: 0.468).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.1/10 (raw 0.606). Shared vocabulary: [appropriate, platforms, technical]. DDaT-only terms [accepted, account, achieve, architectural, architecture, areas] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, ai, capability, cost, data, ecosystem] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.849 (P=0.826, R=0.873). R exceeds P by 0.047 — the WIDAI KSA's 25 words are well-captured by the DDaT element's 108 words, but the DDaT element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance:
NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "You can:
- ensure technical architecture, including programme platforms and shar" and "Ability to select appropriate technical tools, languages, and platforms for data" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.466 < STS 0.606 (gap -0.140). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [accepted, account, achieve] and [ability, ai, capability] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.1%. Shared tokens: [appropriate, platforms, technical]. DDaT-only: [accepted, account, achieve, architectural, architecture, areas]. WIDAI-only: [ability, ai, capability, cost, data, ecosystem].
